what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard ledger entry that documents the L5 propose/accept wire for RHM decision updates, declaring the accept route built but end-to-end unverified and the user-facing proposal/accept UX held for Tim's design walk-through.
observations: Maps an 'accept-update' verb to POST /api/decision/update/accept with {id, ts}
The bearer token rides the #1b operator-session interceptor
It is verifyMode-guarded and triggers gallery:rerender on success
Built against fork's route and a proposed dispatch contract (commit e3b9c84)
The propose half is described as inert RHM-propose; accepted updates fold into compose_definition
User-facing surfacing of proposals and accept/reject UX is explicitly held for Tim's walk-through
A /api/decision/proposals read endpoint is noted as closing an earlier 'invisible proposals' gap
The file candidly states nothing about L5 is verified-by-use
standouts: End-to-end visual verification was never confirmed
User-facing UX is deliberately held for Tim's walk-through before landing
Honest 'scaffolding awaiting bounce and Tim's design' framing
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, timestamps, history
Markdown body sections: STATUS, LANE, ABOUT, KIND, SUMMARY, CLAIMS/DECISIONS, RELATIONS, OPEN_QUESTIONS/GAPS, SOURCE_ADDRESS
Uses board:// cross-references and bracketed status tags like [attempted-unverified]
Cites commits, thread IDs, and doc names as source addresses
concerns: Claims the wire is built and typechecks while admitting no end-to-end verification
User-facing hold appears to have been added after technical build, suggesting face was scaffolded before design approval
Relies on multiple unverified external pieces (#1b interceptor, DNA Accept-button, fork routes) without confirmed integration
The 'Honest' disclaimer signals a trust debt that must be resolved before shipping
notes: A ledger card tracking an unverified L5 accept-update wire that is technically scaffolded and deliberately paused so Tim can shape the user-facing proposal/accept UX before it ships.
questions: What exactly are RHM and compose_definition in this context?
What does verifyMode-guarded enforce in practice?
Which component owns the Accept-button render and what is DNA's role?
What is the coordinated verify-bounce process and who triggers it?
What are the acceptance criteria for Tim's walk-through?
How does the #1b operator-session interceptor differ from other interceptors?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the built propose/accept wire and its held status; the content matches, while also acting as a risk/dependency tracker and a self-flag for unverified work.
apparent_themes: User-facing design gating by a named stakeholder
Separation of reversible engine implementation from UI face
intention_signals: It is trying to record a specific delivery (the L5 accept-update wire) while being transparent that it is not verified and that the visible proposal/accept UX must be shaped by Tim before release, preventing premature shipping.
novelty: The explicit 'held for Tim's walk-through' operating-loop gate and the candid 'attempted-unverified' framing rather than claiming the feature is done.
contribution: This file contributes a transparent checkpoint that separates reversible engine implementation from user-facing UX, captures unverified state, source commit, dependencies, and the Tim walk-through gate so a fused system can decide whether to verify, redesign, or discard the wire without losing context.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard ledger item item-b000495e records the L5 propose/accept update wire for RHM decisions, noting it is built against fork routes and a dispatch contract, guarded by an operator-session interceptor, end-to-end unverified, and held for Tim's user-facing design walk-through.
intention_for_embedding: To document the current state, dependencies, and explicit hold of an assistant-proposed/operator-accepted decision-update feature so it is not mistaken as verified or ready to ship.
